steps.title: Use the data directly
steps.content: The maximum height gives the vertex $(4,324)$, so the model has the form $f(t)=a(t-4)^2+324$. Use the initial height point $(0,68)$ to find $a$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(t)=-16(t+324)^{2}-4$ — vertex would be $(-324,-4)$, not $(4,324)$. Eliminate.
  • B) $f(t)=-16(t-324)^{2}+4$ — vertex would be $(324,4)$, not $(4,324)$. Eliminate.
  • C) $f(t)=-16(t+4)^{2}-324$ — vertex would be $(-4,-324)$, not $(4,324)$. Eliminate.
  • D) $f(t)=-16(t-4)^{2}+324$ — vertex $(4,324)$ and $f(0)=68$. Keep.
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
